Name: Oddish
Flavor-Text: Its scientific name is Oddium wanderus. It is said to cover distances as far as 1,000 feet when night falls, walking on its two roots.

Name: Gloom
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn’t drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.

Name: Vileplume
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The bud bursts into bloom with a bang. It then starts scattering allergenic, poisonous pollen.

Name: Mega Heracross ex
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does that much more damage.
Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Lotad
Flavor-Text: The leaf on its head is naturally dirt repellent and will stay clean even after transporting Pokémon that are covered in mud.

Name: Lombre
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prefers waterfronts with plentiful food. It became nocturnal so it wouldn’t have to compete for food with bird Pokémon.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have any [G] Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There are structures throughout its whole body that produce energy when hit by sound waves with a cheerful rhythm.

Name: Genesect
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

Name: Nymble
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it’s in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

Name: Lokix
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

Name: Charmander
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: The flame on its tail shows the strength of its life-force. If Charmander is weak, the flame also burns weakly.

Name: Charmeleon
Flavor-Text: When it swings its burning tail, the temperature around it rises higher and higher, tormenting its opponents.

Name: Mega Charizard X ex
Effects: Discard any amount of [R] Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Moltres
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are stories of this Pokémon using its radiant, flame-cloaked wings to light up paths for those lost in the mountains.

Name: Darumaka
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

Name: Oricorio ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, if you have any [R] Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched [R] Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charcadet
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: Discard 4 Basic [R] Energy cards from your hand. If you can’t discard 4 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

Name: Seel
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The protrusion on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick ice.

Name: Dewgong
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s [R] or [W] Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it’s colder.

Name: Swinub
Flavor-Text: It searches for food by digging into the ground with its snout. Even frozen ground doesn’t give it any trouble.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Covered by a shaggy coat, it is resistant to the cold. Its tusks of ice thicken when it snows.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.

Name: Suicune
Effects: If you have at least 4 [W] Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees in half with a single hit.

Name: Rotom ex
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has “Rotom” in its name may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached. If this Ability goes away, discard Pokémon Tools from those Pokémon until only 1 remains on each.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yamper
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

Name: Boltund
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

Name: Pawmo
Flavor-Text: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

Name: Pawmot
Effects: You may have this Pokémon also do 60 damage to itself and make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

Name: Misdreavus
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

Name: Mismagius ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent’s Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, their new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snubbull
Flavor-Text: In truth, it is a cowardly Pokémon. It growls eagerly in order to hide its fear from its opponent.

Name: Granbull
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is actually timid and easily spooked. If attacked, it desperately flails its limbs about in an attempt to repel its opponent.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [P] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Mega Diancie ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mimikyu
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

Name: Milcery
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The more sweet aromas it absorbs, the more its body swells. Milcery will deflate when its energy level drops.

Name: Alcremie
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: Desserts that Alcremie have decorated with their cream have a rich, sweet flavor and bring happiness to all who eat them.

Name: Zacian
Effects: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King’s Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

Name: Bramblin
Effects: Place 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.

Name: Brambleghast
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Confused.
Flavor-Text: It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon that has “Tauros” in its name that has any damage counters on it.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a muscular body and excels at close-quarters combat. It uses its short horns to strike the opponent’s weak spots.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It builds its nest on a steep cliff. When it is done gliding, it hops along the ground back to its nest.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: If it succeeds in catching even a faint breeze properly, it can circle the globe without flapping once.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It patiently awaits prey inside its funnel- shaped nest.

Name: Vibrava
Flavor-Text: Rather than using its underdeveloped wings for flight, it rubs them together, emitting ultrasonic waves to attack its enemies.

Name: Flygon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. You may also use this Ability if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon. Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

Name: Gastly
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Mega Gengar ex
Ability-Effect: If 1 of your [D] Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon ex, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. The effect of Shadowy Concealment doesn’t stack.
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

Name: Sableye
Effects: If you have any Stage 2 [D] Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Carvanha
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.

Name: Mega Sharpedo ex
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Seviper
Ability-Effect: If you have any [D] Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

Name: Absol
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Because of this Pokémon’s ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

Name: Krokorok
Flavor-Text: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 160 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

Name: Toxel
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel’s body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

Name: Toxtricity
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic [D] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

Name: Eternatus
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

Name: Empoleon ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Togedemaru
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When it’s in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

Name: Duraludon
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Duraludon’s body is comprised of a special metal that’s lightweight and scratch resistant. It’s also smooth, as though it was given a mirror finish.

Name: Archaludon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand, and your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Attach an amount of Basic Energy up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Once a Smeargle reaches adulthood, it will have other members of its species leave paw prints on its back.

Name: Zigzagoon
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

Name: Linoone
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, and if you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

Name: Mega Lopunny ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 170 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Battle Cage
Description: Prevent all damage counters from being placed on Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) by effects of attacks and Abilities from the opponent’s Pokémon. (Damage from attacks is still taken.)
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Blowtorch
Description: You can use this card only if you discard a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand.Discard a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon, or discard a Stadium in play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Dawn
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, a Stage 1 Pokémon, and a Stage 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Dizzying Valley
Description: Confused Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) don’t recover from that Special Condition when they evolve or devolve.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Firebreather
Description: Search your deck for up to 7 Basic [R] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Grimsley’s Move
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put a [D] Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck. You can’t use this card during your first turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Jumbo Ice Cream
Description: Heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon that has 3 or more Energy attached.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Punk Helmet
Description: If the [D] Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Sacred Charm
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have an Ability (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Wondrous Patch
Description: Attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have any [G] Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

Flavor-Text: Known as the Desert Spirit, this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s dangerous for Wooper to travel alone. They line up in groups of three or four and help each other as they walk around the wetlands.

Name: Toxtricity
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic [D] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched [D] Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Firebreather
Description: Search your deck for up to 7 Basic [R] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Grimsley’s Move
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put a [D] Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck. You can’t use this card during your first turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Punk Helmet
Description: If the [D] Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Sacred Charm
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have an Ability (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ignition Energy
Description: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.If this card is attached to an Evolution Pokémon, it provides [C][C][C] Energy instead.

Name: Mega Charizard X ex
Effects: Discard any amount of [R] Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom ex
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has “Rotom” in its name may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached. If this Ability goes away, discard Pokémon Tools from those Pokémon until only 1 remains on each.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Sharpedo ex
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Lopunny ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 170 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Dawn
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, a Stage 1 Pokémon, and a Stage 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mega Charizard X ex
Effects: Discard any amount of [R] Energy from among your Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
